name: workflow-designer
description: Meta-workflow that designs Mediforce WorkflowDefinitions. Create a new workflow from a natural language idea, or load and edit an existing one. Steps, transitions, and execution config are generated by AI agents with human review loops. The result is a validated, registered WorkflowDefinition ready to run.
preamble:
## Domain Context
This workflow generates structured workflow definitions for the Mediforce platform. All clinical and pharma terminology in workflow descriptions refers to domain modeling constructs.

steps.name: Create or Edit
steps.description: Choose whether to design a new workflow from scratch or load and edit an existing one.

params.name: mode
params.description: create-new — design from scratch; edit-existing — modify a registered workflow

steps.name: Describe Workflow Idea
steps.description: Describe the workflow you want to create in natural language. Include the goal, the steps involved, any review/approval loops, and triggers (manual, scheduled, webhook).

params.name: idea
params.description: Natural language description of the workflow to design

params.name: workflowName
params.description: Machine-friendly name for the workflow (kebab-case, e.g. invoice-review)

steps.name: Fetch Available Workflows
steps.description: Fetch the list of registered workflows from the platform API for editing.

steps.name: Select Workflow to Edit
steps.description: Select the workflow you want to edit from the list of registered workflows.

steps.name: Describe Changes
steps.description: Describe the changes you want to make to the selected workflow. The agent will modify the workflow structure based on your description.

params.name: changes
params.description: Natural language description of the changes to make to the workflow

steps.name: Generate Steps
steps.description: Generate or modify workflow structure (steps, transitions, triggers) based on the baseline and instructions. Outputs YAML with step definitions, routing logic, and review loops — but no execution config yet.

steps.name: Review Steps
steps.description: Review the generated workflow structure. Approve if the steps, transitions, and review loops match your intent. Revise to regenerate with feedback.

steps.name: Describe Execution Preferences
steps.description: Provide guidance for how steps should be executed. For example: which steps should always be human, preferred AI provider (Anthropic, DeepSeek), budget constraints, autonomy level, or any other execution preferences the agent should consider.

params.name: preferences
params.description: Execution preferences and constraints (optional — agent will propose defaults if empty)

steps.name: Generate Execution Proposals
steps.description: Generate 1-N execution proposals for the approved workflow structure. Each proposal assigns executor type, agent config, autonomy level, and plugin to every step. Proposals vary by automation strategy (e.g. all-human, hybrid, full-auto, budget-friendly). Outputs an options array for selection review.

steps.name: Review Execution Proposals
steps.description: Review the proposed execution configurations. Select the one that best fits your needs, or revise to regenerate with different preferences.

steps.name: Validate WorkflowDefinition
steps.description: Combine the approved steps with the selected execution config into a complete WorkflowDefinition and validate it against the WorkflowDefinitionSchema. Returns structured errors if invalid.

steps.name: Register WorkflowDefinition
steps.description: Register the validated WorkflowDefinition in the Mediforce platform. For new workflows this creates v1; for edits the platform auto-increments to the next version.
